LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION AND CHIEF OF POLICE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSES AND WILL DISCLOSE AT THE HEARING THAT SHE KNOWS AND IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 24, 2013 AT 9:30 A.M. PLAINTIFF FILED A LATE OPPOSITION ON JUNE 7, 2013. DEFENDANT IS REQUESTED TO FILE A REPLY BRIEF NO LATER THAN JUNE 18, 2013. IF PARTIES CAN AGREE TO A HEARING DATE LATER THAN JUNE 24, 2013, THEY MAY SUBMIT A REQUEST TO CONTINUE THE HEARING TO AGREED UPON DATE. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO ARGUMENT HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION & CHIEF OF POLICE'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S UNVERIFIED 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE AN ENFORCEABLE MANDATORY DUTY. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. SF CHARTER 4.127 DOES NOT REQUIRE DEFENDANT TO PROCESS PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION. THE CHARTER'S LANGUAGE IS DISCRETIONARY AND SIMPLY STATES THAT THE COMMISSION MAY APPOINT PATROL SPECIAL POLICE OFFICERS. SIMILARLY, THE COMMISSION'S INTERIM RULES AND PROCEDURES USE DISCRETIONARY LANGUAGE. SEE SEC. 1.04. LASTLY, NONE OF THOSE RULES IS AN "ENACTMENT" WITHIN THE MEANING OF GOVERNMENT CODE 810.6. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT & SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MR. GRAY HAS NOT PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE HIS CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL TORT, VIOLATION OF CITY CHARTER SECTION 4.127, AND SFPD'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE APPLICATION PROCESS. MR. GRAY USES THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM COMPLAINT, BUT HE HAS NOT INCLUDED FACTS TO SUPPORT EACH CAUSE OF ACTION TO THE COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

OPPOSITION TO MTN TO STRIKE PLTF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND REQ. TO AMEND SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRAY, JON

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSES AND WILL DISCLOSE AT THE HEARING THAT SHE KNOWS AND IS SOCIAL FRIENDS WITH DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY ELIZABETH SALVESON. JUDGE MILLER PRACTICED LAW IN THE SAME FIRM AS MS SALVESON FROM DECEMBER 1981 - MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992 THROUGH 1995. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT JUDGE MILLER'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. JUDGE MILLER DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THESE FACTS WOULD REASONABLY HAVE A DOUBT THAT SHE WOULD BE ABLE TO BE IMPARTIAL. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION AND SAN FRANCISCO CHIEF OF POLICE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PURSUANT TO CCP 472(B), THE TIME TO AMEND RUNS FROM THE SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF ORDER, UNLESS SERVICE IS WAIVED IN OPEN COURT. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT PLAINTIFF WAIVED SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF ORDER. DEFENDANTS DID NOT SERVE THE NOTICE OF ORDER UNTIL SEPTEMBER 13, 2013. (SEE DECLARATION OF HECIMOVICH IN SUPPORT OF MOTION, EXH. A.) PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS THUS TIMELY FILED ON SEPTEMBER 12, 2013. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST, RAISED IN OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION TO STRIKE, TO FILE A FURTHER AMENDED COMPLAINT. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO AMEND THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, HE MUST FILE A SEPARATE MOTION REQUESTING THAT RELIEF. (COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JON GRAY, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
RUTH BOND, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION, SAN FRANCISCO CHIEF OF POLICE.

Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Judge Miller does not believe that a person aware of these facts would reasonably have a doubt that she would be able to be impartial.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION AND SAN FRANCISCO CHIEF OF POLICE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Pursuant to CCP 472(b), the time to amend runs from the service of the notice of order, unless service is waived in open court. Defendants have not established that Plaintiff waived service of the notice of order. Defendants did not serve the notice of order until September 13, 2013. (See Declaration of Hecimovich in Support of Motion, Exh. A.) Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint was thus timely filed on September 12, 2013. The Court does not address Plaintiff's request, raised in opposition to this motion to strike, to file a further amended complaint. If Plaintiff wishes to amend the Second Amended Complaint, he must file a separate motion requesting that relief. (Counsel for defendants to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: JON GRAY, 415.279.0398, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PROPRIA PERSONA;
ATTY. LARRY HECIMOVICH, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, 415.554.3933, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

No argument held. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION & CHIEF OF POLICE's DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S UNVERIFIED 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege an enforceable mandatory duty. The amended complaint fails to state a cause of action. SF Charter 4.127 does not require defendant to process plaintiff's application. The Charter's language is discretionary and simply states that the Commission may appoint patrol special police officers. Similarly, the Commission's Interim Rules and Procedures use discretionary language. See Sec. 1.04. Lastly, none of those rules is an "enactment" within the meaning of Government Code 810.6. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2013 Continued To Jun-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION and CHIEF OF POLICE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she knows and is social friends with Deputy City Attorney Elizabeth Salveson. Judge Miller practiced law in the same firm as Ms Salveson from December 1981 - March 1987, and from 1992 through 1995. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The hearing is continued to June 24, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. Plaintiff filed a late opposition on June 7, 2013. Defendant is requested to file a reply brief no later than June 18, 2013. If parties can agree to a hearing date later than June 24, 2013, they may submit a request to continue the hearing to agreed upon date. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry Hecimovich, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3933, for defendant and moving party;
Jon Gray, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf (no card).

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested. DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT & SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustained, with 30 days leave to amend. Mr. Gray has not pled sufficient facts to allege his causes of action for intentional tort, violation of City Charter Section 4.127, and SFPD's failure to comply with the application process. Mr. Gray uses the Judicial Council form complaint, but he has not included facts to support each cause of action to the complaint. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
